cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to figure out the correct answer for the question: If parents have trouble with their relationship, the child ___. Looking at the passage provided, I'll try to find the relevant part. The passage says: "When parents are going through difficulties in their own relationship the child sometimes develops guilt. The child can hold himself or herself responsible for the breakdown in relationship between the parents." So according to the text, when the parents have problems, the child might feel guilty. The passage also mentions that this guilt isn't spoken out by the child, but it's something that needs to be explained to them. Now looking at the options: (A) will communicate with their parents – The passage says the child won't speak out their guilt, so communication might not happen. This might not be the right answer. (B) will be responsible for the trouble – The passage says the child can hold themselves responsible, but it doesn't say the child actually is responsible. So this option might be a bit of a trick. The wording is "will be responsible," but the passage says the child feels responsible, not that they are the cause. (C) will feel guilty about the trouble – This matches exactly with what the passage says. The child develops guilt and holds themselves responsible. (D) will help the parents recover – The passage
